sourceMovie.title: Attraction
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Attraction for the same reason it broke through in 2017: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a romance rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Fyodor Bondarchuk keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Upside Down
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Attraction clicked for you, Upside Down (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Attraction?
faq.answer: Try Upside Down, The Discovery, Starman—each shares DNA with Attraction while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Attraction?
faq.answer: Attraction (2017) is best described as Drama, Romance, Science Fiction, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Attraction part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Attraction Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
